findings.summary: Multiple case reports and pharmacokinetic studies document substantial drops in tacrolimus trough levels, in some cases precipitating acute transplant rejection, after solid-organ transplant recipients began taking St. John's Wort.
findings.citation: Mai et al., Ann Intern Med 2000

findings.summary: Hyperforin, the principal active constituent of St. John's Wort, is a potent ligand for the pregnane X receptor (PXR), driving transcriptional upregulation of CYP3A4 and P-glycoprotein expression.

Transplant pharmacy guidance consistently lists St. John's Wort as contraindicated in patients on calcineurin inhibitors (tacrolimus, cyclosporine) because of the well-established risk of subtherapeutic drug levels and rejection.
